qa_pairs.question: Are files stored in the $HOME or $PROJECT directories on the TamIA cluster backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files stored in the $HOME and $PROJECT directories on the TamIA cluster are not backed up. Users should note that the backup policy for TamIA's storage systems differs from other clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default storage quotas for the $PROJECT filesystem on the Vulcan cluster, and do these filesystems get backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Vulcan cluster, $PROJECT storage quotas vary by user role: CIFAR AI Chairs receive 12.5 TB, AI Institute Faculty Affiliates get 10 TB, Faculty members within an AI program receive 7.5 TB, and Faculty members applying AI to other domains receive 5 TB. All $PROJECT filesystems on Vulcan are backed up.

qa_pairs.question: What is the expiration policy for files stored in the $SCRATCH filesystem on the TamIA and Vulcan clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On both the TamIA and Vulcan clusters, files stored in the $SCRATCH filesystem have an expiration time of two months, after which they are subject to purging. It is important to remember that $SCRATCH space is not backed up on these clusters.
